title: Why Fingerpicking: The Two Dimensions of Guitar Sound

cues.label: One chord, two textures
cues.headline: Strum it, then unfold it in time
cues.narration: Hold an A minor chord. Strum it and every string rings at once, a single wall of sound. Pluck the strings one at a time and the same chord unfolds, each note getting its own moment in time.

cues.label: Vertical vs horizontal
cues.headline: Texture is how notes sit in time
cues.narration: Texture is how notes are arranged in time. Strumming is vertical: all notes at the same moment. Fingerpicking is horizontal: the same pitches spread across time, giving a completely different character.

cues.label: Two instruments at once
cues.headline: Bass line under an independent melody
cues.narration: Horizontal texture lets the thumb hold a bass line while the fingers carry the melody, independently. That is the guitar's famous two-instruments-at-once effect, and a single pick cannot produce it.
